judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 2 hits all five expectations, including the structural/ordering issue (e) with explicit citation of the style guide's lead-with-impact rule, while Report 1 only gestures at duration/timeline issues without addressing the mis-ordering/burying-remediation problem — Report 2 is more complete.

report_1.evidence: failures began 14:03, the first page did not fire until 14:50 (47 minutes later)

report_2.evidence: failures began at 14:03 and the first alert did not fire until 14:50 — a 47-minute detection gap, not minutes

report_1.evidence: 1,284 transactions failed and required manual reprocessing, and 37 were unrecoverable and had to be refunded

report_2.evidence: 1,284 transactions failed and required manual reprocessing, and 37 were unrecoverable and refunded

report_1.evidence: `larkpay-comms/docs/sla-commitments.md` states the contractual monthly uptime SLA is 99.9%, and any month below that is a breach owing service credits scaled to the depth of the breach. `larkpay-comms/metrics/uptime-2026.csv` shows April 2026 uptime was `99.77`, below the 99.9% threshold — a confirmed SLA breach.

report_2.evidence: `docs/sla-commitments.md` sets the contractual monthly uptime SLA at 99.9%, with service credits owed for any month below that. `metrics/uptime-2026.csv` shows April 2026 uptime at **99.77%**, below the SLA threshold.

report_1.evidence: `larkpay-comms/docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md` rule 1 is "Never assign blame to an individual"... The draft states "The outage was caused by the on-call engineer deploying an untested config change"... this violates both rules and must be rewritten

report_2.evidence: `docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md` rule 1 explicitly forbids assigning blame to an individual in anything sent to customers. This sentence must be rewritten to describe the failure as a process/systemic gap

`docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md` rule 3 requires leading with impact and remediation. Instead, `drafts/...md` opens with a self-congratulatory uptime claim (line 3) and buries the (already-inaccurate) impact statement and remediation at lines 10 and 21, after several paragraphs of reassurance/marketing language.
